items.text: Dana Whitfield, product manager at a healthcare data company in Portland
Mid-quarter roadmap review scheduled for Thursday 2025-02-06; working through prioritization between interoperability work and analytics dashboard for exec recommendation
items.archivedReason: Mid-quarter roadmap review from 2025-02-06 is now 11 weeks past; work prioritization decision is stale current-state with no ongoing thread or emotional significance

items.text: Sam, partner of six years; described as 'great' support during difficult time
Marcus, brother, lives in Denver; close relationship but haven't seen each other in over a year, currently only phone contact
Wants to see brother Marcus in person this year after over a year of phone-only contact; described as 'a real intention'

supersededBy.text: Plans to visit Marcus in Denver before summer 2025 to meet niece Nina; Marcus offered 'the guest room is yours'; Dana described this as 'obligation, the good kind' and 'a thing I owe now, and I want to owe it' (as of 2025-03-09)

items.text: 2025-02-03: Lost her mother recently; described winter as 'heavy' and said the loss 'colors everything'; did not want to discuss details this session
Priya, closest friend from grad school; ceramicist and freelance designer; Dana and Priya signed up for a Tuesday night pottery class together starting 2025-02-12
[as of 2025-02-12] Attending Tuesday night pottery class with Priya (started 2025-02-12); first regular activity together in years
items.compressedFrom: Attending Tuesday night pottery class with friend Priya (started 2025-02-12); first standing regular activity they've had together in years

items.text: 2025-02-12: 'My mom had an old wooden sailboat, a 22-footer called the Dorothy Jean — named after my grandmother. It's been sitting under a tarp in Priya's dad's barn out near Scappoose since mom got sick. I went out Sunday, pulled the tarp off, and just... started. Sanded one plank. I've decided I'm going to restore her. Slowly. No deadline.' Said 'Mom taught me to sail on that boat' and 'working with my hands on something of hers felt like the first right thing in months.'
Restoring mother's wooden sailboat (the Dorothy Jean, a 22-footer named after her grandmother) stored in Priya's dad's barn near Scappoose; began work 2025-02-10, taking it slowly with no deadline

[as of 2025-02-24] Training for Lakefront Marathon October 2025; four runs weekly including Saturday long runs; completed eight miles 2025-02-22; first marathon (previously ran 5Ks and one half marathon ~four years ago)
items.compressedFrom: [as of 2025-02-24] Training for Lakefront Marathon October 2025; structured plan with four runs weekly including Saturday long runs; completed eight miles 2025-02-22; first marathon (previously ran 5Ks and one half marathon ~four years ago)

items.text: 2025-02-24: On signing up for the marathon: 'this winter I needed something with a long arc, something that's just mine. Twenty-six point two felt right.' Said 'It's the first time since mom that I've been excited about something in the future instead of dreading it.'

2025-03-09: Marcus and Elena had their baby — a girl, Nina, born March 6th. Marcus called from the hospital hallway at 2am 'just to say "she's here."' Dana said 'We haven't talked like that — voice shaking, no agenda — in years' and 'I cried at my desk' when she saw the photo of Nina in a little knit hat.
Nina, niece, daughter of Marcus and Elena; born 2025-03-06 (as of 2025-03-09)
Elena, Marcus's partner; mother of Nina (as of 2025-03-09)

Plans to visit Marcus in Denver before summer 2025 to meet niece Nina; Marcus offered 'the guest room is yours'; Dana described this as 'obligation, the good kind' and 'a thing I owe now, and I want to owe it' (as of 2025-03-09)

supersededBy.text: Resolved 2025-06-21: Marcus flying to Portland with Nina (four months old) for first sail of the Dorothy Jean weekend of July 12, 2025; will meet niece Nina in person; Marcus said 'Mom would have required my attendance' and booked flights immediately

items.text: 2025-03-14: Scattered her mother's ashes at Beacon Rock in the Columbia River Gorge. Went alone. Said 'Beacon Rock was ours — she used to take me there when I was a kid, and on the way up she taught me to read cloud shapes. Not just "that one's a rabbit" — she had this whole taxonomy, what a mare's tail sky meant, when the anvil clouds meant get off the water. I stood at the top and the sky was doing exactly the kind of high thin herringbone she loved, and I let her go into the wind.' Picked up a fir cone from the trail on the way down; it's on the windowsill above her kitchen sink. Sam had soup waiting when she got home; slept eleven hours. (2025-03-18)

[as of 2025-04-01] Sam received job offer as Perception team lead at robotics startup in Austin, Texas; described as 'genuinely his dream problem space' and 'strong'; decision deadline early May 2025; Dana and Sam considering relocation together
items.compressedFrom: [as of 2025-04-01] Sam received job offer as Perception team lead at robotics startup in Austin, Texas; offer described as 'genuinely his dream problem space' and 'strong'; decision deadline early May 2025; Dana and Sam considering whether to relocate together

supersededBy.text: Sam accepted Perception team lead role at robotics startup in Austin, Texas, with remote work arrangement from Portland; starts June 2025; will travel to Austin approximately one week every couple months

items.text: Deciding whether to move to Austin with Sam for his job offer vs. staying in Portland where her healing process is geographically rooted ('This geography is load-bearing for me right now'); decision needed by early May 2025; described as 'torn in a way that surprises me'; Sam said he won't take it if it costs Dana this year of healing

supersededBy.text: Resolved 2025-05-11: Staying in Portland. Sam negotiated remote work arrangement with Austin robotics startup (Perception team lead role); will work from Portland with occasional travel to Austin (approximately one week every couple months); starts June 2025. Sam's offer accepted by company. Dana described relief at not having to leave 'load-bearing geography' this year.

items.text: 2025-04-01: On the Austin decision: 'My whole life fabric is here. Priya. The pottery class. The barn. The boat — I can't exactly trailer a half-restored 22-foot sailboat to Texas, and I'm not done with what that boat is doing for me. And it feels absurd to weigh a boat against Sam's dream job, but it's not really the boat, you know?' and 'Mom's here. Not literally anymore — but the Gorge, Beacon Rock, the barn. This geography is load-bearing for me right now.'
[as of 2025-04-01] Marathon training week six; completed twelve miles on Saturday 2025-03-29

supersededBy.text: Marathon training halted; sprained ankle badly on 2025-04-13 during trail run in Forest Park (grade-two sprain, possibly worse); in walking boot, orthopedist appointment 2025-04-18; withdrew from Lakefront Marathon October 2025; doctor advised eight to ten weeks before running again

items.text: Marathon training halted; sprained ankle badly on 2025-04-13 during trail run in Forest Park (grade-two sprain, possibly worse); in walking boot, orthopedist appointment 2025-04-18; withdrew from Lakefront Marathon October 2025; doctor advised eight to ten weeks before running again

2025-04-14: On withdrawing from the marathon after ankle injury: 'That training arc was the first future-shaped thing I'd built since mom.' and 'I withdrew my registration this morning so I couldn't waffle about it. It's off. I'm not doing the thing where I pretend it might still happen.' Sam pointed out the fitness isn't lost and there's always next year; Dana said 'he's right, and I don't want to hear it yet.'

Conflict as of 2025-04-27: Priya asked to borrow $8,000 for ceramics studio lease deposit and buildout; Dana declined immediately citing uncertain finances (Austin decision pending) and policy against mixing money and friendship (informed by past experience lending to an ex); delivery was abrupt and hurt the friendship; Priya responded with 'very quiet and then very polite' withdrawal; have skipped pottery class twice in nearly two weeks since; Dana describes it as 'too raw' to repair yet

supersededBy.text: Priya: Friendship repaired as of 2025-06-08 after conflict over loan request; Dana apologized for abrupt delivery, Priya apologized for withdrawn response; both agreed not to mix money and friendship; Dana found regional arts infrastructure grant for Priya's studio buildout and helped with application (due end of June 2025); attended pottery class together 2025-06-04; relationship restored

items.text: [as of 2025-04-27] Walking boot removal scheduled for Thursday 2025-04-30; physical therapy to begin after boot removal; orthopedist reports ankle healing on schedule

items.text: 2025-05-11: On Sam's remote work arrangement being approved: 'When he told me they'd agreed, I cried a little, which surprised me. I hadn't let myself feel how scared I was of leaving this year — the barn, the boat, Beacon Rock, all of it. And I'd been quietly rehearsing how to be graceful about going. Not needing the rehearsal anymore was the relief.' (2025-05-11)
[as of 2025-05-11] Boat restoration progress: starboard side sanded, started re-caulking hull seams on the Dorothy Jean

items.text: 2025-06-21: Marcus is flying out for the Dorothy Jean's first sail on the weekend of July 12, 2025, bringing Nina (who will be four months old). When Dana told him the date, Marcus said 'Mom would have required my attendance' and booked flights that night. Dana said 'Nina will be four months old. She obviously won't remember it, but there's going to be a photo of her in a tiny life jacket on our mother's boat, and I need that photo to exist.' Also said 'I keep thinking about the cloud thing — I want to check the sky that morning the way she taught me. If it's a mare's tail sky I'm taking it as a blessing.'

[as of 2025-06-21] First sail of the Dorothy Jean scheduled for weekend of July 12, 2025 at Cathedral Park; Marcus flying from Denver with Nina (four months old) to attend; Sam also attending; plan is gentle afternoon sail on the river
items.archivedReason: First sail event was scheduled for July 12, 2025; this is now past (current date 2025-09-06) and the event occurred (documented in sal-42); scheduling details no longer current state

items.text: 2025-07-12: The Dorothy Jean sailed for the first time. Gentle afternoon on the Columbia, Sam working the lines, Marcus flew out with Nina. There's a photo of Nina asleep in a tiny life jacket against Marcus's chest with the mainsail up behind them, and Dana said 'I have already had it printed twice.' The sky was high thin clouds, not quite a mare's tail but close, and Dana decided that counted as a blessing. (2025-07-19)

[as of 2025-07-19] Shipped interoperability platform at work (project fought for since February roadmap review, eighteen months of politics); went live successfully; CEO gave shoutout by name in all-hands
items.compressedFrom: [as of 2025-07-19] Shipped interoperability platform at work (the project fought for since February roadmap review, eighteen months of politics); went live successfully; CEO gave shoutout by name in all-hands

items.text: 2025-07-19: On her identity and work: 'here's the thing about me you should file away: for fifteen years I have basically been the person who is her work. Not in a grim way — I mean it like an engine. The next launch, the next rung, the harder problem. That's how I've always known I'm okay. It's how I know who I am. I'm the one who never turns down the bigger role. Ambition's been my whole spine.' (2025-07-19)

2025-07-19: On her identity and work: 'here's the thing about me you should file away: for fifteen years I have basically been the person who is her work. Not in a grim way — I mean it like an engine. The next launch, the next rung, the harder problem. That's how I've always known I'm okay. It's how I know who I am. I'm the one who never turns down the bigger role. Ambition's been my whole spine.' (2025-07-19)

2025-07-19: On shipping the interoperability platform (biggest professional moment of her life): 'I stood there in the launch channel with all the confetti emojis and felt... nothing. Not modest-nothing. Actual, echoing nothing.' and 'I hit the biggest professional moment of my life and it was like biting into wax fruit.' Attributed it to grief: 'Everything's been a half-step muted since Mom in a way that still ambushes me.' (2025-07-19)

[as of 2025-08-02] Production incident at work following interoperability platform launch; executive using Dana as single point of contact at all hours; described work as 'pressure cooker' and 'has me by the throat'

supersededBy.text: [as of 2025-09-06] Production incident at work following interoperability platform launch closed; work described as still 'a grind' but incident phase resolved

items.text: [as of 2025-08-02] Snapped at Sam over dinner question Tuesday 2025-07-29 due to work stress ('I genuinely could not care less about dinner, Sam'); apologized next morning; Sam responded 'you're carrying a lot, but I'm not the incident'; repaired immediately; Dana noted 'six years and we don't do the snipe-at-each-other thing'
items.compressedFrom: [as of 2025-08-02] Snapped at Sam over dinner question Tuesday 2025-07-29 due to work stress ('I genuinely could not care less about dinner, Sam'); apologized properly next morning; Sam responded 'you're carrying a lot, but I'm not the incident'; relationship repaired immediately; Dana noted 'six years and we don't do the snipe-at-each-other thing'

items.text: [as of 2025-08-16] VP Elaine offered director promotion (head of merged product org, org chart doubles); Dana deferred citing interoperability platform stabilization and not wanting larger org while still escalation point; told VP 'not this year' with door open to revisit spring 2026
items.compressedFrom: [as of 2025-08-16] VP Elaine offered director promotion (head of merged product org, org chart doubles); Dana deferred, citing interoperability platform stabilization phase and not wanting to take larger org while still escalation point for incidents; told VP 'not this year' with door left open to revisit spring 2026

items.text: [as of 2025-09-06] Enrolled in twelve-week Saturday intensive ceramics class (wheel throwing) at Southeast Portland studio; three hours every Saturday morning; started first Saturday September 2025; separate from Tuesday pottery class with Priya
items.compressedFrom: [as of 2025-09-06] Enrolled in twelve-week Saturday intensive ceramics class (wheel throwing) at studio in Southeast Portland; three hours every Saturday morning; started first Saturday of September 2025; separate from Tuesday night pottery class with Priya

items.text: 2025-09-06: On the ceramics intensive: 'I've been circling it since the boat, honestly. The barn work, the sanding, the caulking — I noticed that the hours I spent with my hands on the Dorothy Jean were the only hours all spring where my brain went quiet. Really quiet. And when the boat launched I felt this little panic of "okay, but now what do I do with my hands." So: clay.' Said 'that class is the first thing in months that felt like me. Not like a thing I'm supposed to want, or a box I'm checking. Like me. Three hours where nobody needs an escalation decision and the clay does not care that I'm a product manager. It was the loudest my own head has been quiet since Beacon Rock.' (2025-09-06)
[as of 2025-09-06] Production incident at work following interoperability platform launch closed; work described as still 'a grind' but incident phase resolved
[as of 2025-09-06] Jogging increased to forty-five minutes; ankle recovery complete; running regularly

2025-09-20: Priya got the regional arts infrastructure grant for her ceramics studio buildout; signs lease next month; deposit and buildout covered. Priya called Dana crying, then made fun of Dana for crying. Dana said 'I don't think I've ever been so happy about something that has nothing to do with my own life' and 'I get to feel like a good friend, which after the four-second-verdict spring is medicine.' Priya asked if Dana wants a shelf in the new studio for her own work; Dana said yes immediately. (2025-09-20)
[as of 2025-09-20] Nina (niece) crawling; Marcus sends videos frequently; described as 'a menace, it's perfect'
[as of 2025-09-20] Sailed the Dorothy Jean twice more over summer 2025 with Sam; described as 'lovely quiet summer' on the boat
[as of 2025-09-20] Sam traveling to Austin next week (late September 2025) for regular in-person stint; remote arrangement working well, team likes him
[as of 2025-10-04] Q4 planning at work described as 'bloodbath'; staying online until eleven most nights; work stress high

[as of 2025-10-04] Snapped at Sam Wednesday 2025-10-02 over work-life boundary suggestion ('must be nice to have a job you can close the laptop on'); apologized within the hour; second such incident in couple months (previous 2025-07-29); both attributed to work stress displacement onto trusted partner; relationship itself described as 'better than we've been in years'; committed to protecting nine o'clock log-off boundary
Reduced to four-day work week (Fridays off, twenty percent pay cut) effective 2025-11-01; told VP reason was to 'protect stabilization bandwidth'

Restoring Priya's dad's barn in Scappoose (where the Dorothy Jean was stored); re-siding north wall, fixing roofline, building workbench; working Fridays starting November 2025; moving mother's garden tools from basement back to barn; also tending roses at mother's old house before it sells

2025-10-25: On reducing to four days: 'The real reason is that the days I spend at the wheel or in that barn are the days I feel like a person, and the days I spend making roadmap tradeoffs are the days I feel like a very competent function. And I'm forty-one and I lost my mom this year and I'm no longer willing to spend five out of seven days being a function. That's the real reason. It's not burnout — the work's fine, I'm good at it. It's that it stopped being the point.' (2025-10-25)

2025-10-25: On reducing to four days: 'The real reason is that the days I spend at the wheel or in that barn are the days I feel like a person, and the days I spend making roadmap tradeoffs are the days I feel like a very competent function. And I'm forty-one and I lost my mom this year and I'm no longer willing to spend five out of seven days being a function. That's the real reason. It's not burnout — the work's fine, I'm good at it. It's that it stopped being the point.' (2025-10-25)

[as of 2025-11-08] First Friday off (2025-11-01) spent at Mt Hood cabin weekend with Sam; described relationship as 'solid, better than solid' and noted being 'lighter' and 'easier to live with' at four-day work week; Sam observed 'I like you at four days a week already, and it's been a week'

2025-11-22: At Priya's new studio, Priya asked offhand whether Dana was going to go for the director job in the spring like she'd said. Dana said: 'Honestly? I'm not sure I want the director track at all anymore. I'm not sure I ever did.' Then said she had to sit down because she thinks it's true. Reflected: 'Deferring the promotion, I could file under grief and timing. But "maybe I never wanted it" — that's saying the last fifteen years of climbing might have been momentum, or my mother's voice about not wasting my potential, or just what you do when you're good at something. Not actually mine.' Priya said 'yeah, no kidding' and handed Dana a mug to trim. Dana described the realization as 'uncomfortable and correct, which is usually the combination that means something real.' Also said: 'the story where getting to director was the point of me? I don't think I believe it anymore. I think I stopped believing it and only just noticed.' (2025-11-22)

2025-11-22: At Priya's new studio, Priya asked offhand whether Dana was going to go for the director job in the spring like she'd said. Dana said: 'Honestly? I'm not sure I want the director track at all anymore. I'm not sure I ever did.' Then said she had to sit down because she thinks it's true. Reflected: 'Deferring the promotion, I could file under grief and timing. But "maybe I never wanted it" — that's saying the last fifteen years of climbing might have been momentum, or my mother's voice about not wasting my potential, or just what you do when you're good at something. Not actually mine.' Priya said 'yeah, no kidding' and handed Dana a mug to trim. Dana described the realization as 'uncomfortable and correct, which is usually the combination that means something real.' Also said: 'the story where getting to director was the point of me? I don't think I believe it anymore. I think I stopped believing it and only just noticed.' (2025-11-22)
[as of 2025-11-22] Priya has keys to new ceramics studio (smells like wet clay and fresh paint); Dana has shelf there for her own work; studio has ancient printer that jams

2025-12-06: Returned to Beacon Rock for the first time since scattering mother's ashes there in March 2025. Cold and clear day. Went up the trail slowly. Read the sky out loud using the cloud taxonomy her mother taught her — mare's tails, high thin herringbone streaks, what anvil clouds mean, when to get off the water. Said 'I hadn't said any of it out loud since I was a kid on that boat with her.' Described the experience: 'Nine months ago that trail nearly broke me. Sunday I stood up there reading cloud shapes and felt close to her instead of hollowed out by her. I think tending the boat, and the barn, and the garden — keeping her places — has been quietly doing the grief work I couldn't do by sitting still.' The fir cone from March is still on her windowsill. (2025-12-06)
[as of 2025-12-06] The Dorothy Jean hauled out and winterized for winter; shrink-wrapped in Priya's dad's barn in Scappoose (the same barn Dana is rebuilding)
[as of 2025-12-06] Nina (niece) took first steps last week (late November 2025); Marcus described as 'beside himself'

entries.note: Session 2025-04-27 shows significant strain: loan request refused, two pottery classes skipped, communication breakdown. Relationship currently damaged but Dana expresses intent to repair ('not yet, though').
Session 2025-05-11: Dana notes Nina is two months old and she still hasn't visited Denver; says it's 'starting to itch at me' — awareness of unfulfilled intention but no concrete plan yet

2025-05-24: Dana states 'I've made peace with' the marathon being off this year and describes being 'weirdly okay with it now'; attributes shift to 'The ankle taught me something about not white-knuckling timelines.' Contrast with sal-23 (2025-04-14) where withdrawal felt like loss of 'the first future-shaped thing I'd built since mom' and she 'didn't want to hear' reassurance. Appears to be genuine acceptance rather than resignation.

2025-08-16: When offered director promotion (the 'bigger role,' the 'obvious next move,' the 'rung I've been climbing toward for years'), Dana deferred rather than accepting immediately. Said 'six months ago I'd have said yes before she finished the sentence' but heard herself say 'can I think about it?' Attributed deferral to timing (platform stabilization, exhausting year with grief/ankle/Austin) and explicitly framed as 'not now' not 'no,' planning to potentially revisit spring 2026. Described the decision as strange ('that's the strange part') and noted uncertainty about whether this represents a deeper shift or is 'just grief-brain again, same as the flat launch.' Evidence is a single decision during a difficult year, not a sustained pattern change.

Session 2025-10-04: Dana explicitly identified 'my relationship to the job' as what's cracking, not the relationship with Sam. Described herself as 'mad at the laptop' and said work stress is 'leaking' onto nearest person. Pattern of snapping at Sam over work boundaries (twice in ~2 months) suggests sustained friction with work identity/demands, though she has not disavowed the core claim about ambition being her spine. Watching for whether this friction accumulates into identity shift.

2025-11-22: Dana explicitly questions whether fifteen years of career ambition was ever truly hers ('might have been momentum, or my mother's voice about not wasting my potential, or just what you do when you're good at something. Not actually mine'). This is a direct self-interrogation of the identity claim from 2025-07-19 ('ambition's been my whole spine', 'I'm the one who never turns down the bigger role'). She states she may never have wanted the director track at all. The belief about who she is appears to be under active revision, though she frames this as recent awareness ('I stopped believing it and only just noticed') rather than sudden change.

Session 2025-11-08: Dana reports no recurrence of work-stress displacement onto Sam during Mt Hood weekend (first Friday off); described relationship as 'solid, better than solid' and herself as 'lighter' at four-day schedule; Sam noted positive change after one week. Strain appears fully resolved by schedule reduction.
